title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council postponed a vote on a $2.65 million bond to replace firefighter gear containing PFAS.
Firefighters are at risk from PFAS in gear, linked to cancer; union leaders urge immediate funding approval.
Councilors cited budget concerns and need for more review time, delaying the decision on PFAS-free gear.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to reduce long-term costs and focus on native wildlife.
The zoo faces $57 million in needed improvements and $900,000 in annual operational costs amid declining attendance and seasonal revenue challenges.
Transition team involving municipal leaders and zoo advocates will form in July to evaluate the proposed changes and ensure community engagement.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown had a last-minute microwedding due to a blizzard in Somerville, Massachusetts.
Meredith Goldstein discusses the evolving use of the term 'partner' in modern relationships.
Yowei Shaw shares her experience of embarrassment over having a male partner on the Love Letters podcast.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, from Burlington, MA, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH.
He honors his late father, treated for glioblastoma at Mass General, and his own testicular cancer survival.
Despite a serious blood infection, Averback resumed training, emphasizing endurance and moving forward.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots safety Kevin Byard works out with potential trade target A.J. Brown in offseason sessions.
Patriots' offer for Eagles WR A.J. Brown was declined; Philadelphia seeks high draft picks in return.
A.J. Brown rumors persist, fueled by workouts with Byard and social media posts by Julian Edelman.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens finished his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Boston Bruins are considering signing prospect James Hagens after BC's season ended with a 4-3 OT loss to UConn.
Hagens, a 2025 NHL Draft seventh pick, is expected to boost the Bruins in their final regular-season stretch.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in 'Is This Thing On?' available on Disney+.
Chuck Norris stars in 'Lone Wolf McQuade', streaming on Tubi, Pluto TV, Plex, Prime Video, and YouTube.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' with Cillian Murphy is now streaming on Netflix.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora, Red Sox manager, is non-committal about his future with the team beyond the 2027 season.
Cora's current contract, worth over $7 million annually, runs through 2027, and discussions on extension have not been disclosed.
Cora is 134 wins away from tying Terry Francona for second-most victories by a Red Sox manager.

title: The Bruins’ second line isn’t just a pleasant surprise. They’ve been one of the NHL’s best this year.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' second line of Arvidsson, Zacha, and Mittelstadt has outscored opponents 35-16 this season in 5-on-5 play.
The Bruins' second line boasts the second-best goal differential in the NHL at +19, only behind Colorado's top line.
Pavel Zacha has scored a career-best 23 goals this season, contributing to the line's success with 51 points in 66 games.

title: He’s running Boston to support kids and families at MGH
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Andrew Mahr, 37, from Needham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Mass General Hospital's pediatric cancer care.
Mahr witnessed young patients' courage at Heartbreak Hill in 2014, inspiring his continued support for pediatric cancer research.
Despite higher need, pediatric cancer receives less funding than adult cancer, prompting Mahr's advocacy and fundraising efforts.
